channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Greg Abbott Threatens to Cut $110 Million From Houston Police Over ICE Policy Dispute
item.description: The office of Gov. Greg Abbott wants the city to stop enforcing a new ordinance governing how its officers engage with ICE.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman
item.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: One of the sponsors of the ordinance accused Gov. Greg Abbott, above, of “attempting to defund the police.”

item.title: U.S. Is Negotiating an Iran Deal That Would Buy Time, Again
item.description: The United States proposed a 20-year “suspension” of all nuclear activity, even as President Trump demands assurances that Iran can never build a nuclear weapon.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Jacquelyn Martin/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance arriving at Joint Base Andrews from Islamabad, Pakistan, on Sunday. Mr. Vance there were “some good conversations” with Iran in Pakistan, and the ball is now in Tehran’s court.

item.title: U.S. Military Strikes Another Boat in Pacific, Killing 2
item.description: The attack was the 49th since the campaign against vessels in the Caribbean and the eastern Pacific started in early September.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The U.S. Southern Command, led by Gen. Francis L. Donovan of the Marine Corps, announced the strike on Monday.

item.title: Michigan Man Is Released in Wife’s Disappearance in the Bahamas
item.description: Brian Hooker had been in police custody since April 8 after he said his wife fell off their boat and was swept away by strong currents.
item.dc:creator: Christine Hauser

item.title: Trump and MAGA Embraced Orban. Now, They’re Worried by His Loss in Hungary’s Election.
item.description: Viktor Orban, the Hungarian prime minister, and his right-wing playbook were embraced by parts of the American right. Now some are worried by his defeat.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning and Shane Goldmacher
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Many of President Trump’s supporters have seen Prime Minister Viktor Orban of Hungary as a kindred spirit, and as an incubator of ideas that they embraced.

item.title: Vance Says Pope Leo Should Stay Out of U.S. Affairs
item.description: The vice president, a Catholic, defended President Trump, who had accused the pope of being too liberal and “weak on crime.” The pontiff has said that he has “no fear of the Trump administration.”
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Jacquelyn Martin
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance in Pakistan on Sunday.

item.title: Tony Gonzales, a Texas Congressman, Says He Will Resign
item.description: The third-term Republican lawmaker is accused of coercing a staff member into a sexual relationship.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Tony Gonzales during a House Committee on Homeland Security hearing in Washington in February.

item.title: What Happens in California Governor’s Race After Eric Swalwell Stepped Down?
item.description: For Democrats, the drama is reverberating well beyond the state.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Eric Swalwell of California

item.title: What the U.S. Blockade of Iran and the Strait of Hormuz Might Look Like
item.description: The U.S. military has provided few details on how it might carry out President Trump’s orders as he seeks to pressure Tehran on a peace deal. But history and established practices offer some clues.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: A ship in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Oman on Sunday.

item.title: Eric Swalwell Says He Will Resign From Congress After Sex Abuse Accusations
item.description: The California lawmaker said in a social media post Monday that he would quit. He has denied accounts of abusing women.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold, Jill Cowan and Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Eric Swalwell of California had been in a crowded field vying to succeed Gov. Gavin Newsom.

item.title: Harvard Says Trump Lawsuit Rehashes a Battle the School Already Won
item.description: The government has accused Harvard of harboring antisemitism. Harvard says it prevailed in a similar case last year, and wants the lawsuit moved to the judge who ruled in its favor.
item.dc:creator: Mark Arsenault
item.media:credit: Lucy Lu for The New York Times
item.media:description: Harvard sued the federal government last year when the school’s funding was threatened. Now the federal government is suing the school.

item.title: Police Arrest Woman Accused of Stalking Lindsey Buckingham of Fleetwood Mac
item.description: The guitarist, Lindsey Buckingham, had obtained a restraining order against the woman two years ago, but the police said she continued to follow him in March.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:credit: Chad Batka for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lindsey Buckingham performing in New York in 2011. A woman who is accused of stalking the former Fleetwood Mac guitarist was arrested over the weekend in Indiana.

item.title: Stepbrother of Anna Kepner Charged With Murdering Her on Carnival Cruise
item.description: The 16-year-old boy, identified only as T.H. in court papers, was charged with murder and sexual abuse in the death of Anna Kepner, 18.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson

item.title: Luna Calls for Quick Expulsion of Swalwell as Ethics Panel Opens Inquiry
item.description: Representative Anna Paulina Luna, Republican of Florida, said she wanted votes as early as Wednesday on expelling Representatives Eric Swalwell and Tony Gonzales over sexual misconduct accusations.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Trump’s Explanation for an Image of Himself as Jesus: ‘I Thought It Was Me as a Doctor’
item.description: The image showed President Trump bathed in divine light and clad in religious robes. His interpretation was that the image depicted him as a doctor, not Jesus Christ.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump said on Monday that he had thought the image he had posted to his Truth Social account had depicted him not as Jesus — but as a physician.

item.title: Prosecutors Investigate ICE Agents Who Detained ChongLy Scott Thao in Minnesota
item.description: ChongLy Scott Thao, a naturalized U.S. citizen, was briefly detained during a federal immigration crackdown in Minnesota in January.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Londoño
item.media:credit: Leah Millis/Reuters
item.media:description: Immigration agents in January removed ChongLy Scott Thao from his Minnesota home while he was wearing shorts and a blanket.

item.title: Trump’s Erratic Behavior and Extreme Comments Revive Mental Health Debate
item.description: As the president threatens to wipe out Iran and attacks the pope, even some former allies and advisers are questioning whether he has grown increasingly unbalanced, describing him as “lunatic” and “clearly insane.”
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Donald J. Trump in Charlottesville, Va., last week.

item.title: Trump Attack on Pope and Posting of Jesus Image Draw Widespread Criticism
item.description: A prominent conservative bishop was among the Catholic leaders who jumped to the pope’s defense.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Dias
item.category: United States Conference of Catholic Bishops
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Luca Zennaro
item.media:description: “I have no fear of the Trump administration, or speaking out loudly of the message of the Gospel, which is what I believe I am here to do,” Pope Leo said.

item.title: Man Arrested After U.S. Military Aircraft Damaged in Ireland
item.description: A man was arrested on Saturday and accused of criminal damage at Shannon Airport in western Ireland, police said.
item.dc:creator: Ali Watkins
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Leon Neal/Getty Images
item.media:description: A C-130 Hercules seen at R.A.F. Fairford in March. The same type of plane was damaged at Shannon Airport on Saturday by a man who entered a restricted area, Ireland’s police force said.

item.title: World War II Veterans Say NATO Is Imperfect but Necessary
item.description: President Trump’s stance on NATO has upended the norms that the service of World War II veterans helped to establish. Some say the partnership could be revamped for modern times.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Mokam
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Swalwell’s Fall Punctuates Woes of Democrats in California, of All Places
item.description: Democrats have struggled to find a candidate for governor who can capture the imagination of voters in one of their most important strongholds.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher
item.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
item.media:description: The California governor’s race has attracted a wide range of Democratic candidates. A gathering in San Francisco in February drew, from left, Tony Thurmond, Matt Mahan, Tom Steyer, Betty Yee, Eric Swalwell, Xavier Becerra, and Katie Porter. Mr. Swalwell dropped out of the race on Sunday.

item.title: Iran Blockade Sets Up a Test of Which Side Can Endure More Pain
item.description: President Trump is trying to choke off the country’s lifeline with a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. But the Iranians are betting that his tolerance for political pain is limited.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Jenny Kane/Associated Press
item.media:description: Fuel prices reflected on a semi-truck in Aurora, Ore., last week. “Soon you’ll be nostalgic for $4 to $5 gas,” Iran’s top negotiator warned American consumers after peace talks fell apart over the weekend.

item.title: Trump Takes Down Post Depicting Himself as a Jesus-Like Figure
item.description: The image, which President Trump shared on Truth Social shortly after criticizing Pope Leo, depicted him as a divine leader healing the sick.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump has posted several apparently A.I.-generated images of himself over the last year, sometimes drawing significant backlash.

item.title: For Months, Pope Avoided Clash With Trump. Now He’s Clapping Back.
item.description: President Trump lashed out on Sunday night at Pope Leo XIV, who responded hours later by saying he would continue to oppose war.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Andrew Medichini/Associated Press
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV arriving in Algeria on Monday. He told reporters that he was not afraid of the Trump administration after the president criticized him on Sunday.

item.title: After Charlie Kirk’s Death, a Fight for the Youth Vote
item.description: A fledgling Democratic group and the far-right white nationalist Nick Fuentes seek to make gains on college campuses that have recently been dominated by Turning Point USA.
item.dc:creator: Robert Draper
item.media:credit: Photographs by Jamie Kelter Davis and Ariana Drehsler for The New York Times
item.media:description: Nicholas J. Fuentes, left, the 27-year-old white nationalist, and Zee Cohen-Sanchez, the founder of the Democratic organization National Ground Game, are competing against Turning Point USA for young voters in what will be the first election since Charlie Kirk’s assassination.

item.title: Trump’s Military Transgender Ban Is Keeping Workers on Expensive Paid Leave
item.description: Highly trained service members have been put on paid leave for nearly a year as they wait for the military to decide their fate.
item.dc:creator: Dave Philipps
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Landon Edwards for The New York Times
item.media:description: Katie Benn, an Army air defense officer, hasn’t been able to do her job in nearly a year, sidelined because she is transgender.

item.title: The Last Day at Kids of Faith: Parents Navigating a Child Care Crisis
item.description: Inside one of the hundreds of day care centers that have recently closed in one state, forcing parents to make difficult decisions about their careers.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Yee and Erin Schaff
item.media:description: Aimee Aranda comforted her daughter, Zennett, at Kids of Faith Place on the day care’s last day.
